heading: “Building” defined

notes: References in TextThe National Capital Transportation Act of 1960, referred to in par. (4), is , , , which enacted sections 651, 652, 661 to 665, and 671 of former Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works, and enacted provisions set out as notes under section 651 of former Title 40 and which was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 86–66974 Stat. 537Pub. L. 91–143, § 8(a)(1)83 Stat. 322July 14, 1960Dec. 9, 1969The National Capital Transportation Act of 1965, referred to in par. (4), is , , . Section 1 of the Act, which was classified to a note under section 681 of former Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works, was repealed by , , . Section 2 of the Act, which was classified to section 681 of former Title 40, has been omitted from the Code. Sections 3 and 4 of the Act, which were classified to sections 682 and 683, respectively, of former Title 40, were repealed by , , . Sections 5(a) (no subsec. (b) was enacted) and 6 of the Act, which were classified to sections 684 and 685, respectively, of former Title 40, were repealed by , , . Section 7 of the Act amended provisions classified to section 662 of former Title 40, which was repealed by , , . (2) “, after construction or alteration in accordance with plans and specifications of the United States” after “”.Pub. L. 94–541August 12, 19681970—Par. (4). added par. (4).Pub. L. 91–205
Short Title, , , which enacted this chapter, is popularly known as the “Architectural Barriers Act of 1968”.Pub. L. 90–48082 Stat. 718Aug. 12, 1968

Applicability of 1976 Amendment to Leases Entered Into Before, On, or AfterJanuary 1, 1977, , , provided that:Pub. L. 94–541, title II, § 20290 Stat. 2508Oct. 18, 1976“The amendment made by paragraph (1) of section 201 of this Act [amending this section] shall not apply to any lease entered into before . It shall apply to every lease entered into on or after , including any renewal of a lease entered into before such date which renewal is on or after such date.”January 1, 1977January 1, 1977

children.text: to be constructed or altered by or on behalf of the United States;
to be leased in whole or in part by the United States after ;August 12, 1968

to be financed in whole or in part by a grant or a loan made by the United States after , if such building or facility is subject to standards for design, construction, or alteration issued under authority of the law authorizing such grant or loan; orAugust 12, 1968

to be constructed under authority of the National Capital Transportation Act of 1960, the National Capital Transportation Act of 1965, or title III of the Washington Metropolitan Area Transit Regulation Compact.
